beats.text: Here's our two-by-two matrix — entries a, b on top, c, d below.

The determinant comes from permutations. First permutation: one, two — take entry a from row one, column one, and entry d from row two, column two. An arrow traces each selection diagonally. Product: a times d.
Second permutation: two, one — take b from row one, column two, and c from row two, column one. Arrows cross in an X. Product: b times c.
Now assign signs. Permutation one, two is even — plus sign. Permutation two, one is odd — minus sign. The formula assembles: a-d minus b-c.
That's the determinant. Two permutations, two products, two signs — combined into one number that captures everything about this matrix.
